purpose: Series-level rolling-window tracker for NBA playoff postmortems, LiveFlow entries, edge calls, misses, and patch requirements.

folder_map.note: Game 1 PHI_NYK and MIN_SAS postmortems were confirmed by user screenshot inside nba_postmortems, so the index points to that folder for those files while finalized rolling-window game files are registered under Backtest_Logbook/NBA/Rolling_Window_Series/.

games.rolling_window_use: Context only; apply blowout deweighting due to PHI rest/fatigue disadvantage.
High-weight Game 3 input; close-game mechanics and Embiid-out rotation effects are more predictive than Game 1 margin.
High-weight Game 4 input; Knicks closing power and Sixers offensive fragility with limited Embiid are now primary forecast variables.
Use rebound tracking inputs, but cap confidence where Game 2 blowout minutes risk is possible.
High-weight Game 3 input for blowout-risk detection and Spurs pace/transition response; low-weight for normal competitive total projections due to garbage-time distortion.
High-weight Game 4 input. Wembanyama closeout scoring, guard-rebound 4Q decay, and live-under efficiency kill-switch become active forward filters.

rolling_window_weighting_notes: Game 1 established Lakers offensive suppression without Luka and OKC margin control.
Game 2 showed the danger of halftime team-total unders when LAL already reached 58 and playoff starter-extension remained alive.
Primary forward-looking flags: LAL turnover volatility, OKC second-half pressure, OKC bench green-light scoring, playoff under buffer requirement.

games.rolling_window_use: Track Lakers offensive suppression without Luka and OKC margin control profile.

High-weight Game 3 input for Lakers turnover/scoring volatility and OKC second-half pressure. Low-weight for clean under projections because late-game and bench scoring distorted the suppression profile.
Track Detroit turnover pressure and Cavs late-game response profile.
High-weight Game 3 input for Detroit pressure continuity and Cavs live team-total risk filters; medium-weight for raw total projection because Cleveland still reached 97 despite poor 3P shooting.

active_patch_queue.trigger: Spread expands beyond 20 by halftime or 28 by end of 3Q; downgrade rebound overs and starter-dependent props.
Blowout plus bench 3PA rate and low defensive resistance; reduce confidence in full-game live unders.
Knicks live spread inside 5 in playoff 4Q with Brunson on floor and opposing closer fatigue detected.
Prior blowout caused by opponent short-rest/fatigue disadvantage after Game 7.
Cleveland live team total over requires at least neutral 3P form or a clear foul-extension path after 3Q.
Detroit leading or tied in final six minutes with Cunningham plus Robinson/Harris spacing active.
Harden below 40% FG with multiple turnovers entering second half.
No playoff team-total under unless model median is at least 3.5 points below the live line.
If target team has 55+ points at halftime, require clear pace decline plus FTA suppression before under entry.
For playoff spreads between 8 and 18 entering Q4, assume primary scorers remain active until under 4:00 unless lead reaches 20+.
When OKC bench scorers are active late, downgrade full-game and team-total unders because bench 3PA remains aggressive.
If SAS leads by 1-7 entering final 6:00 and Wembanyama is active despite foul trouble, widen SAS margin distribution by +1.5 to +2.5 points.
For guard rebound ladders needing one board in 4Q, downgrade if elite bigs/long forwards are dominating defensive rebounding share and game is close.
If 3Q combined scoring exceeds 60 with one team shooting 50%+ and late-game foul path is live, force cashout/no-add on full-game unders.
Downgrade McDaniels/Randle rim and short-mid efficiency when Wembanyama is active and generating 4+ stocks or strong paint deterrence.

workflow_rule: Every finalized NBA postmortem should be registered either from the active nba_postmortems folder or the standard Backtest_Logbook/NBA folder, then indexed inside this rolling-window series file for forward Game N+1 forecasting.
